title: Mock Frontend Interview — Five Rounds
description: Complete five timed mock interview rounds back-to-back under real interview conditions. Round 1: JavaScript (30 min). Round 2: React (30 min). Round 3: Machine Coding (60 min). Round 4: Project Discussion (45 min). Round 5: Debugging (30 min). No AI. No Google. Official docs only during Round 3. This is your graduation simulation.
requirements: Round 1 — JavaScript (30 min): answer questions on closures, event loop, async/await, promises, objects, arrays, map/filter/reduce, debounce, this/call/bind/apply
Round 2 — React (30 min): answer questions on JSX, components, props, state, useEffect, useMemo, useCallback, useRef, Context, Redux, RTK Query, routing, performance
Round 3 — Machine Coding (60 min): build ONE randomly assigned project — Todo, Product Dashboard, Kanban, Movie Search, or Weather Dashboard
Round 3 constraints: TypeScript, responsive, API integration, loading/error/empty states, clean architecture — no AI, no Google, official docs only
Round 4 — Project Discussion (45 min): explain FrontForge architecture, JSON renderer, folder structure, design decisions, challenges, improvements
Round 4 also covers your Day 23 refactored project — defend architecture and trade-offs
Round 5 — Debugging (30 min): fix React bugs, performance issues, TypeScript errors, and routing problems in provided scenarios
Complete self-evaluation rubric: JavaScript, React, TypeScript, Redux, Performance, Machine Coding, Project Discussion, Communication (1–5 stars each)
Verify final graduation checklist: fundamentals, Redux, RTK Query, routing, APIs, performance, debugging, machine coding, portfolio, resume, LinkedIn
acceptanceCriteria: All five rounds completed within their time boxes — use a timer
Round 1: can trace async code and explain event loop order without notes
Round 2: explains Context vs Redux vs RTK Query with trade-offs unprompted
Round 3: MVP ships in 60 minutes with loading, error, and empty states
Round 3: folder structure explainable in 60 seconds
Round 4: FrontForge JSON renderer and block registry explained clearly
Round 4: names three honest trade-offs and three future improvements
Round 5: all four debugging categories addressed with documented root cause
Self-evaluation completed honestly — no category left blank
Graduation checklist verified — ready to apply for frontend roles
hints: Schedule breaks between rounds — 5 minutes to reset mimics real interview loops.
Round 1–2: answer out loud even if alone — communication is scored.
Round 3: same MVP-first strategy as Day 21–22 — types, static UI, API, polish.
Round 4: open FrontForge repo side by side — point to actual files while explaining.
Round 5: narrate reproduce → isolate → fix → verify for each bug.
If a round goes poorly, finish it anyway — recovery under pressure is interview skill.

bonus.title: Graduation with distinction
bonus.description: If all core rounds complete with self-eval average 4+ stars.
requirements: Record full mock and review communication filler words
Write a post-mortem: one strength and one gap per round
Update GitHub README on best project with architecture section from Day 23
Send one application within 48 hours of graduation

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — Infinite Rendering
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Round 3 submission freezes browser; thousands of renders in Profiler
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect sets state without dependency array, or setState in render body
debuggingScenarios.fix: Add correct deps; move setState to event handler; guard effect with condition

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — Performance Bug
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Search input laggy; every keystroke re-renders entire list of 200 items
debuggingScenarios.cause: Unmemoized list items; parent state update re-renders all children; missing debounce
debuggingScenarios.fix: Debounce search state update; React.memo on list item; useMemo filtered list

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — TypeScript Error
debuggingScenarios.symptom: useParams returns string | undefined; component crashes accessing .id without guard
debuggingScenarios.cause: Assumed route param always present; missing null check after useParams
debuggingScenarios.fix: Guard: if (!id) return <NotFound />; narrow type before use

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — Routing Issue
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Detail page blank on refresh; works on client navigation only
debuggingScenarios.cause: SPA hosting missing fallback to index.html; or Route path mismatch with Link
debuggingScenarios.fix: Align Route and Link paths; configure host rewrite; add catch-all NotFound route

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — State Mutation
debuggingScenarios.symptom: UI does not update after edit; React DevTools shows state changed but render skipped
debuggingScenarios.cause: Direct mutation: items[i].title = newTitle instead of immutable map update
debuggingScenarios.fix: setItems(prev => prev.map(item => item.id === id ? { ...item, title } : item))

debuggingScenarios.title: Round 5 — API Failure Uncaught
debuggingScenarios.symptom: White screen when API returns 500; console shows unhandled promise rejection
debuggingScenarios.cause: fetch without try/catch; assuming non-ok response throws automatically
debuggingScenarios.fix: try/catch async; check response.ok; setError with user-facing retry UI
